chunks.page_summary: This page provides guidance on integrating Rheonics Smart Module Electronics (SME) with a Codesys-based controller using the Profinet communication protocol, detailing involved products, communication overviews, and prerequisites.
product_names: Rheonics inline viscometer and density meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Profinet communication through Codesys

Modified on: Tue, 31 Jan, 2023 at 10:29 PM

What products are involved?
This article is based on the use of the SME (Smart Module Electronics) from SRV (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/), SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/), DVP (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-gas-lng-density-meter-dvp/) and DVM (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/). Hence it can be used for any of these Rheonics sensors.

What is the purpose of this article?
To give guidance on the integration of SME with a controller based on Codesys using a Profinet communication protocol.

1. General concepts

1.1 PROFINET overview
PROFINET is one of the most commonly used industrial communication protocols, it enables data exchange between controllers and devices based on international standards. To link all the components in its network, PROFINET requires standard Ethernet as the communication medium. It can operate on higher transmission speeds than other protocols and requires names and IP addresses for all devices.

1.2. CODESYS overview
CODESYS (Controller Development System) is a manufacturer-independent software for industrial automation technology. It follows the IEC-61131-3 standard to program intelligent devices and controllers. It is compatible with multiple device types and controllers. You can see which companies offer CODESYS-compatible devices here (https://www.codesys.com/the-system/codesys-inside.html).

1.3. Prerequisite:
*   CODESYS software installed.
*   Rheonics Sensor Electronics (SME) with Ethernet and PROFINET enabled.
*   Rheonics GSD files (https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000401423-profinet-gsd-file-for-rheonics-inline-viscometer-and-density-meter)
*   IP address of the Rheonics SME - this can be read out from the SME using RCP software.
*   CODESYS-compatible controller.

1.4. SME PROFINET connection
key_facts: The article provides guidance on integrating Rheonics Smart Module Electronics (SME) with a Codesys-based controller using the Profinet communication protocol.
PROFINET is a commonly used industrial communication protocol that enables data exchange between controllers and devices based on international standards.
PROFINET requires standard Ethernet as the communication medium and uses names and IP addresses for all devices.
CODESYS (Controller Development System) is a manufacturer-independent software for industrial automation technology.
CODESYS follows the IEC-61131-3 standard for programming intelligent devices and controllers.
Prerequisites for integration include CODESYS software, Rheonics SME with Ethernet and PROFINET enabled, Rheonics GSD files, the IP address of the Rheonics SME, and a CODESYS-compatible controller.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of PROFINET communication for Rheonics Smart Module Electronics and outlines the configuration steps for establishing a PROFINET network using Codesys.
chunks.extracted_text:
The Smart Module Electronics from Rheonics is PROFINET prepared if ordered. For that, the Ethernet RJ45 port is used. It can be connected directly to the client's PC or through a router, switch or hub.

Figure 1: PROFINET Communication Diagram.

2. Configuration steps

i. Select the controller device
With a Project created on Codesys, follow the next steps to set the controller and establish a Profinet Network.

1. Double-click on the Device (previously selected when creating the Project).
2. Click on "Communication Settings".
3. Select the Gateway by default.
4. Click on “Scan Network” after selecting the gateway, this will open a pop-out window.
5, 6. With the scan process, the controller connected to the network should be shown. Select it and click OK.
key_facts: The Smart Module Electronics from Rheonics supports PROFINET communication if ordered.
The Ethernet RJ45 port is used for PROFINET communication with the Smart Module Electronics.
The Smart Module Electronics can be connected directly to a client's PC or through a router, switch, or hub.
Configuration steps involve using Codesys to set up the controller and establish a PROFINET Network.
Key steps include double-clicking the device, accessing "Communication Settings", selecting the Gateway, scanning the network, and selecting the controller found.

key_facts: The document illustrates the process of setting up a controller, specifically a Raspberry Pi, within the CODESYS development environment.
The initial configuration involves selecting 'Device (CODESYS Control for Raspberry Pi SL)' and navigating to 'Communication Settings'.
A network scan is performed to locate and identify the Raspberry Pi controller.
The controller is identified by its device name 'raspberrypi', address '0033', and other details like serial number and target ID.
The system uses UDP as the block driver and supports TLS for encrypted communication.
After the device is added, a detailed overview of its properties, including target vendor (3S - Smart Software Solutions GmbH) and target version (4.4.0.0), is displayed.
The document notes that an option can be enabled to update received values without explicitly calling parameters in the program.

key_facts: To configure variable updates in CODESYS, navigate to 'PLC Settings' and choose 'Enabled 2 (always in bus cycle task)' under 'Always update variables'.
Selecting 'Enabled 2 (always in bus cycle task)' for variable updates will prevent login without a subsequent download.

The process to add an Ethernet adapter involves right-clicking 'Device' in the project tree, selecting 'Add Device', navigating to 'Fieldbuses' > 'Ethernet Adapter', selecting 'Ethernet', and clicking 'Add Device'.
The Ethernet adapter being added is from '3S - Smart Software Solutions GmbH', with version 3.5.17.0, described as 'Ethernet Link'.

After adding the Ethernet adapter, it must be double-clicked in the project tree, and its 'General settings' require verification that the 'eth0' adapter's IP address matches the controller (e.g., PLC).

Figure 7: IP Addresses for slaves.

3. Install GSD files on Codesys
Download the GSD files from Rheonics Support Page (https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000401423-profinet-gsd-file-for-rheonics-inline-viscometer-and-density-meter) and install them in the CODESYS Device Repository with the next steps:
1. Go to "Device Repository" in the Tools Menu.
2. Select the "Edit Locations" option.
3. Click on "Add" and indicate the path to the GSD files on the PC, write a name for the file, and click OK.

key_facts: PROFINET slaves can be configured with specific IP addresses, subnet masks, and default gateways.
The PN_Controller supports I/O Provider/Consumer status settings, including substituting values if the application stops and adding to I/O mapping.
Input data substitution can be configured to use 'Zero' or 'Last valid value' when the application stops.
Rheonics GSD files for inline viscometers and density meters are available from the Rheonics Support Page and are necessary for integration with CODESYS.
GSD files are installed in CODESYS via the 'Device Repository' in the 'Tools' menu, by adding a new location and specifying the file path and name.
warnings_notes: The CODESYS messages pane indicates: Total 0 error(s), 1 warning(s), 2 message(s).

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions on how to scan for and add Rheonics devices within the CODESYS software, verify device network parameters, and access detailed device parameters.
chunks.extracted_text:
5,6. Right-click on “PN_Controller” and select “Scan for Devices”.
7. Wait some seconds until the software detects all connected devices (SME). If you have one or multiple devices connected, you can click “Copy All Devices to Project” or individually select the ones you want to add and click “Copy to Project”.

Figure 9: Scan for devices.

Upon successful addition of the devices, they will appear in the Project’s tree. Verify each device has a unique IP address, station name and MAC address on the network. IP and MAC addresses on each device should be the same as the ones shown on the RCP software.

The station name is set by Rheonics for each sensor and can’t be changed. It has a standard format of: “Sensor Type-Sensor Serial Number”, for example: “SRV-B07-0102”, where SRV is the sensor type and B07-0102 is the sensor SN.

You can now download and run the project. With the program running, the read values should appear in the PNIO Module I/O Mapping Section of the added devices. Device Parameters show the 23 Parameters with their 5 measurements ( https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000393235-parameter-list-access-for-field-devices https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000393235-parameter-list-access-for-field-devices https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000393235-parameter-list-access-for-field-devices https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000393235-parameter-list-access-for-field-devices see the list https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000393235-parameter-list-access-for-field-devices here https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000393235-parameter-list-access-for-field-devices ).

key_facts: To add devices in CODESYS, users must right-click 'PN_Controller' and select 'Scan for Devices'.
After scanning, users can add devices to the project by clicking 'Copy All Devices to Project' or by selecting specific devices and clicking 'Copy to Project'.
Upon successful device addition, it is necessary to verify that each device's unique IP address, station name, and MAC address on the network match those displayed in the RCP software.
Rheonics pre-configures the station name for each sensor in the format 'Sensor Type-Sensor Serial Number', which cannot be changed.
For example, in 'SRV-B07-0102', 'SRV' indicates the sensor type and 'B07-0102' is the sensor's serial number (SN).
Once the project is running, read values for devices should appear in the PNIO Module I/O Mapping Section.
Device Parameters in the software include 23 Parameters with 5 measurements.
Identified devices show a Vendor-ID of 0x002A and a Device-ID of 0x0202.
The product 'RH HT-Coll-4 SRV' has a Product ID of 0x0001, and its vendor is Rheonics GmbH with a Vendor ID of 0x0644.

key_facts: The document provides troubleshooting steps for PROFINET communication with Rheonics devices.
It demonstrates viewing real-time process parameters like viscosity, density, temperature, and kinematic viscosity within the CODESYS environment.
Rheonics offers support articles for PROFINET diagnostics and output connectivity.
The CODESYS project named 'Rheonics Profinet.project' is connected to a 'CODESYS Control for Raspberry Pi SL' device.
The project shows 0 errors and 0 warnings during build and runtime.
The current parameter values for density (median and average) are 1.1 (REAL), and temperature median is 23.577 (REAL).
diagram_labels: Device (connected) [CODESYS Control for Raspberry Pi SL]
